title: Analyzing the two-dimensional doped Hubbard model with the Worldvolume HMC method
comment: 10 pages, 3 figures, contribution to the 42nd International Symposium on Lattice Field Theory (LATTICE2025), 2-8 November 2025, Mumbai, India
summary: We apply the Worldvolume Hybrid Monte Carlo (WV-HMC) method [arXiv:2012.08468] to the two-dimensional Hubbard model, which is known to suffer from a severe sign problem when the system is doped (away from half filling). We show that the method predicts physical observables with controlled statistical errors on an $8 \times 8$ lattice at temperature $T/t = 1/6.4 \approx 0.156$ and interaction strength $U/t = 8.0$ ($t$ is the hopping amplitude), for which the standard determinant quantum Monte Carlo fails.

AI.tldr: The Worldvolume Hybrid Monte Carlo (WV-HMC) method is successfully applied to the two-dimensional doped Hubbard model, overcoming the sign problem and providing physical observables with controlled statistical errors on an 8x8 lattice at specific temperature and interaction strength.
AI.motivation: The motivation of this paper is to address the severe sign problem in the doped two-dimensional Hubbard model, which hinders the use of standard determinant quantum Monte Carlo methods for certain parameter ranges. The authors aim to demonstrate the effectiveness of the WV-HMC method in such scenarios.
AI.method: The researchers apply the Worldvolume Hybrid Monte Carlo (WV-HMC) method to the two-dimensional Hubbard model. This method is used to compute physical observables with controlled statistical errors for a system that is away from half-filling, where traditional quantum Monte Carlo methods typically fail due to the sign problem.
AI.result: The result of this study is that the WV-HMC method can predict physical observables with controlled statistical errors for the 8x8 lattice at a temperature T/t = 1/6.4 and interaction strength U/t = 8.0, conditions under which the standard determinant quantum Monte Carlo method fails.
AI.conclusion: The conclusion drawn from the research is that the WV-HMC method is a viable approach for simulating the doped two-dimensional Hubbard model, particularly in regions of parameter space where the sign problem makes conventional quantum Monte Carlo simulations ineffective.
